description_long: This is a pair of vintage, rectangular toolboxes made primarily of weathered wood with metal hardware. Each box features a sturdy, metallic folding handle on the top, attached with visible screws and metal plates. The front face of each toolbox is equipped with a prominent metal clasp latch, designed to secure the lid tightly. The wood surfaces are heavily worn, showing signs of age, scratches, and discoloration, with a patina that suggests long-term use in rugged environments. The sides and back are plain, with the wood grain and aging marks visible throughout. No external branding or labeling is present, and the overall construction appears robust and utilitarian.
description: A pair of weathered wooden toolboxes with metal handles and clasp latches, showing heavy use and patina. The surfaces are rough and aged, with no visible branding or labels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metal clasp latches and the folding metal handles on top of both toolboxes.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rectangular profile and the alignment of the metal handles, with minimal detail on the sides.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden backs and the hinges, with the handles visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the handles and the boxy shape with no additional features.
attribute_handle_type: Folding rectangular steel handles, fixed to the top with metal plates and screws, no visible ergonomic padding.
attribute_latch_mechanism: Front-facing metal clasp latches, likely steel, with a simple flip-and-hook mechanism, no keyholes or combination locks visible.
attribute_compartment_configuration: Single main compartment per box, no visible trays, drawers, or dividers; internal layout not visible but implied to be undivided.
